verdicts.title: Nopriv AJAX handler ai_ajax with no nonce check - information disclosure via ai-get-settings when remote debugging enabled
verdicts.attack_vector: The wp_ajax_nopriv_ai_ajax action (ad-inserter.php:13512) is registered without any nonce check (lines 7179-7180 show the nonce checks are commented out). If the admin has enabled Remote Debugging (non-default setting, DEFAULT_REMOTE_DEBUGGING=AI_DISABLED=0), unauthenticated attackers can POST to admin-ajax.php?action=ai_ajax&ai-get-settings=1 to retrieve the full plugin settings JSON via ai_write_settings_string(). Same path available in ai_wp_hook. The check-page and remote-ads-txt endpoints are also accessible unauthenticated when remote debugging is on.
verdicts.auth_required: none (requires non-default remote debugging config to be enabled by admin)
verdicts.remediation: Uncomment the nonce check at lines 7179-7180 for all requests; or require authentication before exposing settings data. For remote debugging paths that must be unauthenticated, implement a secret-key-based gate similar to the existing ai-secret approach used for AI_URL_DEBUG_POSITIONS.

verdicts.title: eval() on admin-stored ad block code - intentional by-design PHP execution feature
verdicts.attack_vector: class.php:644 contains eval("?>" . $code . "<?php ") where $code comes from AI_OPTION_CODE stored in the database. This is an intentional PHP code execution feature (Process PHP option per block). Access to set ad block code requires manage_options capability. The ai_php_enabled() check prevents execution when DISALLOW_FILE_EDIT or DISALLOW_FILE_MODS is defined. This is documented plugin behavior, not a vulnerability.
verdicts.remediation: No remediation needed for intended functionality. Document that PHP processing requires admin trust.

verdicts.title: Stored XSS in Global Fields admin page - $website_url echoed without esc_url/esc_html
verdicts.attack_vector: On the AI Global Custom Fields admin page (class.php:11623), $website_url from get_option(AI_WEBSITES) is echoed unescaped inside an HTML href attribute. An admin storing javascript: URL in the website URL field would cause XSS when another admin views the page. Admin-to-admin stored XSS. The page is only accessible to users with the configured access role (default: administrator).
verdicts.evidence:
$websites = get_option(AI_WEBSITES, array()); // line 11523
$website_url = $websites[$current_remote_website_index]['url']; // line 11529
...
<a href="<?php echo $website_url; ?>"> // line 11623 - unescaped
verdicts.remediation: Use echo esc_url($website_url) at line 11623. Also apply esc_html() to $website_name at the same line.

verdicts.title: Unescaped HTTP_USER_AGENT in debug output - output within HTML comment, logged-in users only
verdicts.attack_vector: At lines 5121-5127, $_SERVER['HTTP_USER_AGENT'] and $_SERVER['HTTP_ACCEPT_LANGUAGE'] are echoed without escaping in ai_write_debug_info(). The function is only called from ai_shutdown_hook() which requires AI_DEBUG_PROCESSING flag AND the user must be logged in (AI_USER_LOGGED_IN). The debug output is wrapped in HTML comments (<!-- -->) at lines 5566/5568. The REFERRER at line 4952 is parse_url() processed. A crafted User-Agent containing --> could technically break out of the comment, but the preconditions (logged-in + debug mode) make practical exploitation negligible.
verdicts.auth_required: subscriber (logged-in user with debug mode triggered)
verdicts.remediation: Apply htmlspecialchars() to $_SERVER['HTTP_USER_AGENT'] and $_SERVER['HTTP_ACCEPT_LANGUAGE'] for defense-in-depth.

verdicts.title: Settings page XSS flows via integer-derived variables ($start, $block) - integer type prevents XSS
verdicts.attack_vector: The taint analyzer flagged echo $start and echo $block where variables derive from $_GET parameters. However, settings.php line 124 casts to int: $start = (int)$_GET['start']. Integer values cannot carry XSS payloads. echo get_option('gmt_offset') * 3600 * 1000 outputs a numeric multiplication result. All are false positives from taint tracking ignoring type coercion.
verdicts.evidence:
if (isset($_GET['start']) && is_numeric($start) && $start >= 1 && $start <= 96) $start = (int)$_GET['start']; // line 124
...
echo $start; // line 285 - integer, no XSS possible
verdicts.remediation: No remediation needed. Variables are cast to integers before output.

verdicts.title: preg_replace RCE false positive - no /e modifier present
verdicts.attack_vector: The taint analysis flagged $save_url = preg_replace("/&tab=\d+/", "", $save_url) at settings.php:112 as RCE. The regex '/&tab=\d+/' contains no /e modifier and simply strips tab parameters from the URL string. No code execution is possible. This is a pattern-match false positive from the analyzer.
verdicts.evidence:
$save_url = $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']; // line 110
$save_url = preg_replace("/&tab=\d+/", "", $save_url); // line 112 - no /e flag, not RCE
verdicts.remediation: No remediation needed for the preg_replace call itself.

verdicts.title: phpQuery call_user_func_array RCE false positive - internal library with trusted callbacks
verdicts.attack_vector: The taint analyzer flagged call_user_func_array($callback, array($v, $k)) in the bundled phpQuery library. The $callback is supplied by internal plugin code, not user input. $k comes from intval($args) making it an integer. This is a spurious inter-procedural false positive across thousands of lines of library code.
verdicts.evidence:
$k = intval($args); // forced to integer
$r = call_user_func_array($callback, array($v, $k)); // $callback is internal, $k is integer

verdicts.title: Unserialize uses allowed_classes protection - no object injection risk
verdicts.attack_vector: The ai_unserialize() wrapper at ad-inserter.php:13195 uses unserialize($string, ['allowed_classes' => false]) which prevents PHP object injection. The import path at line 8429 requires admin access protected by check_admin_referer(). Class.php lines 4139-4304 build w3tc_code strings using base64_encode(serialize(...)) of plugin-controlled data - not user input.

verdicts.title: Stored XSS - $website_name and connected_website name echoed unescaped in admin page
verdicts.attack_vector: On the Global Fields admin page, $website_name is echoed unescaped as link text (class.php:11623). Additionally, get_transient(AI_CONNECTED_WEBSITE)['name'] is passed unescaped to sprintf() in a success notice (class.php:13009). These are admin-to-admin stored XSS vectors via malicious website name values stored in plugin options.
verdicts.evidence:
echo $website_name; // line 11623, unescaped
echo sprintf(__('Global custom fields on %s saved.', 'ad-inserter'), $connected_website['name']); // line 13009 - name not escaped
verdicts.remediation: Apply esc_html($website_name) at line 11623. Apply esc_html($connected_website['name']) before sprintf() at line 13009.

verdicts.title: SSRF false positive - hardcoded Google OAuth endpoint
verdicts.attack_vector: The taint analyzer flagged wp_remote_post('https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token', ...) in adsense-api.php:85. The URL is hardcoded and not user-controlled. Body parameters come from admin-set options. No SSRF is possible.

verdicts.title: XSS flows in settings.php from json_encode/boolean output in admin context - false positives
verdicts.attack_vector: Multiple taint flows originate from get_option()/get_transient() returning stored plugin settings, flowing to json_encode() output (viewport_data, all_viewports) or boolean expressions (echo $connected ? 1 : 0). These are all in the admin-only settings page context. The viewport names are sanitize_text_field()-processed at line 11573. JSON output in a JavaScript context does not require HTML escaping. Boolean echo statements output only 0 or 1. All are false positives from overly-broad taint tracking.
verdicts.evidence:
$viewport_data []= array('name' => sanitize_text_field($viewport_name), ...); // sanitized
echo json_encode($viewport_data); // JSON context, admin only
echo $connected_website !== false && $connected ? 1 : 0; // outputs only 0 or 1
verdicts.remediation: Low-priority hardening: use wp_json_encode() instead of json_encode() in JS contexts. Not a practical attack given admin-only context.

notes: The plugin's core security posture is adequate: settings save uses check_admin_referer(), settings page requires manage_options, unserialize uses allowed_classes=>false.
ADI-001 is the most significant finding: nopriv AJAX handler lacks nonce verification and exposes plugin settings when non-default remote debugging is enabled by admin.
ADI-003 and ADI-009 are confirmed stored XSS but confined to admin-to-admin context (require admin to set malicious data, only affects admins viewing the page).
The automated tool incorrectly classified preg_replace without /e modifier as RCE, integer-typed variables as XSS sources, and a hardcoded OAuth URL as SSRF.
The eval() at class.php:644 is intentional PHP block processing, admin-only, gated by DISALLOW_FILE_EDIT/DISALLOW_FILE_MODS constants.
No unauthenticated code execution or unauthenticated data modification path was found in the plugin code.
